steps.title: Check the opening modifier
steps.content: The sentence opens with a participial phrase, Forming extensive networks via mycorrhizal association. The subject that follows must be something that can actually form those networks.

steps.title: Reject impossible subjects
steps.content: Choice A begins with it, which has no clear actor. Choice B makes transport the subject, and Choice C makes nutrients the subject. Neither transport nor nutrients form networks.

steps.title: Choose the network formers
steps.content: Pine trees and the fungus Tricholoma matsutake are the organisms that can form networks through their entangled roots and hyphae. The answer is D.
